notes: Maharashtra. Bal Gangadhar Tilak launched his Home Rule Movement in 1916. It was primarily centered in Maharashtra, which was his home base. He had significant influence and following in this region. He published his ideas through his newspapers "Kesari" (in Marathi) and "Mahratta" (in English) from Maharashtra. Q.716. Which of the following organisations, divided into two branches, was established by Lokmanya Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Annie Besant? SSC CGL 11/09/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Indian Association (b) Theosophical society (c) East Indian Association (d) Home Rule League Sol.716.(d) Home Rule League. In April 1916, Tilak founded the Indian Home Rule League, with its headquarters in Poona. The league's first meeting was held in Belgaum. In September 1916, Annie Besant founded the All-India Home Rule League, based in Madras. Organizations and their founders : Indian Association - Surendranath Banerjee and Ananda Mohan Bose; Theosophical Society - Helena Blavatsky, Henry Steel Olcott, William Quan Judge.

Chauri Chaura Incident took place at Chauri Chaura in the Gorakhpur district of United Provinces (now Uttar Pradesh) in British India. The police there fired upon a large group of protesters participating in the non-cooperation movement. In retaliation, the demonstrators attacked and set fire to a police station, killing all of its occupants. The incident led to the deaths of three civilians and 22 policemen. Mahatma Gandhi halted the non-cooperation movement on 12th February 1922 after the Chauri Chaura incident.

Singapore. The Indian National Army (INA), also known as the Azad Hind Fauj, was a collaborationist armed unit of Indian supporters that fought under the command of the Japanese Empire. It was founded by Mohan Singh in September 1942 during World War II.

Jawaharlal Nehru. He was an Indian freedom fighter who later became the first Prime Minister of India. In December 1929, he was chosen as the President of the Indian National Congress during its annual session in History Lahore. At this event, he raised the tricolour flag on the banks of the Ravi River.

Jawaharlal Nehru. On 31 October 1940, Jawaharlal Nehru was arrested for participating in individual Satyagraha, protesting against India’s forced involvement in the Second World War. During this period, Nehru also engaged in limited Civil Disobedience and spent approximately nine years in jail between 1921 and 1945.

Subhas Chandra Bose. Indian National Army (INA) was a collaborationist armed unit of Indian collaborators that fought under the command of the Japanese Empire. It was founded by Mohan Singh in September in Southeast Asia during World War II. Some notable people associated: Mohan Singh, Habib ur Rahman, Lt. Col Hori Lal Verma.

Annie Besant. Annie Besant, an Irish theosophist, started the Home Rule movement in India in 1916, demanding self-government similar to the Irish Home Rule League. She founded the Home Rule League in Madras in September 1916 and established over 200 branches across India. Q.723. Who among the following personalities renounced his knighthood after the Jallianwala Bagh massacre? Matriculation Level 20/06/2024 (Shift - 1) (a) Syed Ahmed Khan (b) CV Raman (c) Rabindranath Tagore (d) JC Bose Sol.723.(c) Rabindranath Tagore. The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred on April 13, 1919, when British troops, led by General Dyer, fired on a peaceful crowd in Amritsar, killing hundreds. In protest, Mahatma Gandhi returned the Kaiser-i-Hind title in 1920. The Hunter Commission was set up in 1919 to investigate the incident.

Chittaranjan Das. The Swaraj Party, initially called the Congress Khilafat Swaraj Party, was established in 1923 by Chittaranjan Das and Motilal Nehru. This formation took place after the Gaya Annual Conference of the Indian National Congress in December 1922. Other important organizations and their founders: Indian National Congress (1885): Founded by A.O. Hume. All India Kisan Sabha (1936): Sahajanand Saraswati. Azad Hind Fauj (1943): Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose. Q.725. Which of the following Satyagrahas was started by Dr B R Ambedkar in 1927? SSC Stenographer 13/10/2023 (Afternoon) (a) Nasik Temple Satyagraha (b) Dharasana Satyagraha (c) Mulshi Satyagraha (d) Mahad Satyagraha Sol.725.(d) Mahad Satyagraha: It is also known as the Chavdar Tank Satyagraha, begun in Mahad (Raigad), Maharashtra. It centered around the Chavdar Tank, a public water source that was reserved exclusively for upper castes.
Sucheta Kriplani (First female Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh from - 67). She sang Vande Mataram on 14th August 1947 in the opening session of Parliament.

Lahori Gate. The National Flag of India was adopted on 22 July 1947, Designed by Pingali Venkayya. Q.728. Who moved the resolution proposing that the National Flag of India be a 'horizontal tricolor of saffron, white and dark green in equal proportion '? SSC MTS 11/09/2023 (1st Shift) (a) Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel (b) Jawaharlal Nehru (c) Mahatma Gandhi (d) Dr Rajendra Prasad Sol.728.(b) Jawaharlal Nehru. The Tricolour was adopted as our national flag at a Congress Committee meeting in Karachi in 1931.
Subhas Chandra Bose - founder of 'Azad Hind Fauj'. 'Tiger Legion' was formed in 1941 with the help of the German Government to fight against the British.

Canada. The Komagata Maru was a coal-transport steamship that had been converted into a passenger ship by Hong Kong-based businessman Gurdit Singh. It set off from Hong Kong in April 1914, reaching Vancouver’s harbour a month later with 376 people on board, most of them Sikhs. The steamship eventually ended up back in India on 29th Sep 1914 at Budge Budge, Calcutta.

Karachi Resolution (1931). The Karachi Session of Congress in 1931 was presided by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. Gandhi Irwin Pact - 5 March 1931. It marked the end of civil disobedience. Q.732. Who among the following freedom fighters was one of the founders of the Fergusson College in Pune in 1885 ? SSC MTS 10/05/2023 (Afternoon) (a) Subhash Chandra Bose (b) Bal Gangadhar Tilak (c) Sardar Vallabh Bhai Patel (d) Lala Lajpat Rai Sol.732.(b) Bal Gangadhar Tilak. Other Co-founding members of Fergusson College :- Vaman Shriram Apte, Vishnushashtri Chiplunkar, Mahadeo Ballal Namjoshi and Gopal Krishna Agarkar.

Non-Cooperation Movement (1920 - 1922). It was organized by Mahatma Gandhi to obtain self governance. Many lawyers gave up their practices such as Motilal Nehru, C.Rajagopalachari, Saifuddin Kitchlew, Vallabhbhai Patel, Asaf Ali, T. Prakasam and Rajendra Prasad.

May 1924. He was publicly executed in Koyyuru on May 7 after being tied to a tree and shot. His final resting place (tomb) is at Krishna Devi Peta, 111 km from Visakhapatnam. In August 1922, he launched the Rampa Rebellion (Manyam Rebellion) against the British. During this rebellion, he was helped by Bobbili Dora. The Prime Minister has unveiled a bronze statue of Alluri Sitharama Raju in Andhra Pradesh to celebrate 125th birth anniversary on 4th July 2022.

1879-1880. The Koya Revolts rose under Tomma Sora during 1879-80. Other important revolts are Kol Mutiny (1831), Ho and Munda Uprisings (1820-1837), The Santhal Rebellion (1855-56), Ramosi Uprising (1822- 1829), etc.
Awadh. He led a peasant movement in Awadh against Talukdars and Landlords.

Vedaranyam. C. Rajagopalachari broke the salt law in 1930 at Vedaranyam, a small coastal town in the then Tanjore District. The first Indian governor of free India was C. Rajagopalachari. Q.738. What was the name of the women's regiment in the Indian National Army founded by Subhash Chandra Bose? SSC CGL 18/08/2021(Evening) (a) Rani of Jhansi Regiment (b) Rani Ahilyabai Regiment (c) Rani Padmawati Regiment (d) Rani of Didda Regiment Sol.738.(a) Rani of Jhansi Regiment. Q.739. Which maharaja of Manipur signed the Instrument of Accession with the Government of India? SSC CHSL 05/08/2021 (Afternoon) (a) Kulachandra Singh (b) Bodhchandra Singh (c) Nara Singh (d) Gambhir Singh Sol.739.(b) Bodhchandra Singh, signed the Instrument of Accession on August 1947, with the assurance of Manipur's internal autonomy. This agreement marked Manipur's entry into the Union of India, though full integration followed with the Manipur Merger Agreement in 1949.
Ram Manohar Lohia. Congress Socialist Party (Socialist caucus within the Indian National Congress) - Founded in 1934. Other founders - Jayaprakash Narayan and Acharya Narendra Dev.

Nagpur. Flag Satyagraha is a part of civil disobedience during the Indian independence movement. Q.742._______was executed at Lahore in November 1915 for his role in the Ghadar Conspiracy in February 1915. SSC CGL 09/03/2020 (Evening) (a) Kartar Singh Sarabha (b) Tantia Mama (c) Maganbhai Patel (d) Vijay Singh Pathik Sol.742.(a) Kartar Singh Sarabha. Ghadar Conspiracy (1915) - Plan to initiate a pan-India mutiny in the British Indian Army during World War by the Ghadar Party (United States), the Berlin Committee (Germany), the Indian revolutionary underground in British India and the German Foreign Office. Socio Religious Reforms Q.743. Consider the following statements with regard to Satnami Movement of Central India. a) The movement was founded by Ghasidas. b) The movement was to improve the condition of leather workers. Which of the above statements is/are correct? SSC Stenographer 10/12/2024 (Afternoon) (a) Only a (b) Both a and b (c) Neither a or b (d) Only b Sol.743.(b) Both a and b. Guru Ghasidas, a 19th-century advocate of the Satnami sect, founded the Satnami community in Chhattisgarh, promoting Satnam (truth) and equality. His teachings share similarities with Hinduism and Buddhism. The Satnami movement was led by non-Brahman leaders who worked to empower marginalized communities.
